narration: In this lesson, the fast pointer reads every value while the slow pointer marks the next open good spot.
callout: At the start, both pointers are at the front.
decision: When fast sees a non-zero value, copy it into slow's spot.

narration: Zeros are skipped, so the slow pointer only moves when we keep a useful number.
callout: Fast found 7, so 7 belongs in the next good spot at index 1.
decision: That packs all non-zero values together at the front.

predictionPrompt.prompt: Should the slow pointer move when fast is looking at a zero?

choices.label: No, keep slow where it is
choices.feedback: Correct. Slow only moves after placing a non-zero value.

choices.label: Yes, move slow too
choices.feedback: Not this time. That would leave holes in the packed values.

narration: After the scan, every slot after the slow pointer gets filled with zero.
callout: The front is packed with useful values and the back is cleared out.
decision: This keeps the non-zero order the same.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why is this called unidirectional traversal?
